response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These responses can lead to symptoms such as hives swelling or difficulty breathing  
Living underwater is not related to these immune system reactions  
Peanut allergies do not affect the ability to live in water  
The body's reaction to allergens is unrelated to environmental conditions such as water or air  

Sec. 2  
People with peanut allergies can live underwater if they are not exposed to peanuts  
The presence of water does not trigger an allergic reaction to peanuts  
Allergic reactions occur when allergens enter the body through ingestion inhalation or skin contact  
Living underwater does not prevent these forms of exposure  
Therefore peanut allergies do not prevent people from living underwater
